global.title: powa-revenue-distribution model captures epoch-based token revenue sharing
global.content:
# powa-revenue-distribution model captures epoch-based token revenue sharing

## Purpose

Models a revenue distribution system where revenue is split proportionally across multiple epochs, each containing investor tokens (iPOWA) and contributor tokens (cPOWA). Each epoch has a weight factor that determines its share of total revenue. The document model provides an off-chain configuration, simulation, and audit layer for what ultimately deploys as Solidity smart contracts.

Source: [NicWickman/powa](https://github.com/NicWickman/powa)

## State Schema

**Core entities (from requirements, not yet architected):**

- **Epoch** — index, iPOWA supply, cPOWA supply, weight factor, status (DRAFT/APPROVED/DEPLOYED/RETIRED), on-chain addresses (post-deploy), label
- **Revenue Configuration** — total amount, revenue token identifier, distribution date
- **Distribution Record** — epoch reference, deposited amount, iPOWA/cPOWA distributed amounts, dust, tx hash, timestamp
- **User Holding** (simulation, likely local scope) — iPOWA/cPOWA balances per epoch, computed claimable
- **Claim Target** — account address, target contract address, target type

Global scope: epochs, revenue config, distribution records
Local scope (candidate): user holding simulations

## Modules

Proposed module split (deferred to Architect):

1. **EpochManagement** — create, edit, reorder epochs; status lifecycle transitions
2. **RevenueConfiguration** — set total revenue, denomination, schedule
3. **DistributionTracking** — record deposits, per-epoch actuals, dust, claim events
4. **HolderSimulation** — define user holdings, compute claimable amounts
5. **TargetConfiguration** — register claim destinations per account

## Key Design Decisions

- Off-chain model represents the *business view* (epoch config, allocations, claims), not Solidity internals (accumulators, rewardDebt)
- Status lifecycle (DRAFT→APPROVED→DEPLOYED→RETIRED) provides a governance gate before irreversible on-chain deployment
- Computed fields (weighted supply, revenue share, claimable) may be derived at render time rather than stored — Architect decides
- This is a **new functional zone** (Token Economics / Revenue Distribution) — does not extend any existing Powerhouse model
- Maximum 20 epochs per document (mirrors on-chain gas limit policy)

---

Relevant Notes:
- [[epochs distribute revenue proportionally by weighted token supply]]
- [[masterchef accumulator pattern enables per-token revenue tracking]]
- [[powa document model bridges on-chain contracts with off-chain governance]]

global.description: Document model for configuring and tracking POWA multi-epoch revenue distribution across iPOWA (investor) and cPOWA (contributor) token holders
